pack: FCEI C10 School Leadership Quality Culture Live Deployment Integration Pack v1

OKM.title: Ministry of Education and Culture, Finland
OKM.use: Policy and system framing: education development is connected to public policy, national education responsibilities, culture, research, governance and long-term development.

EDUFI.title: Finnish National Agency for Education - National Core Curriculum
EDUFI.use: Curriculum framing: national foundation, equality, local curriculum, local needs, school culture and active support for teaching and school activities.

FINEEC_ENHANCEMENT.title: FINEEC - Enhancement-led evaluation
FINEEC_ENHANCEMENT.use: Quality and evaluation framing: development, participation, trust, learning, appreciation, positive approach, impact and self-evaluation.

FINEEC_QUALITY.title: FINEEC - Support for quality management
FINEEC_QUALITY.use: Quality management framing: education providers have responsibility for maintaining and improving the quality of activities.

lms_module.title: School Leadership, Quality Culture and Enhancement-Led Development

sections.short_explanation: This module introduces school leadership, quality culture and enhancement-led development as practical leadership responsibilities within the FCEI Finnish Teacher Training Development Framework. Participants learn how leaders can build trust, shared responsibility, professional dialogue, evidence-informed improvement and learner-centred decision-making across a school, college or education team.
sections.okm_framing: The Ministry of Education and Culture framing positions school development as a system-level responsibility connected to policy, governance, research, culture, equality, institutional direction and the long-term development of education. For FCEI, this means leadership is not limited to administration. Leadership connects classroom practice, curriculum decisions, staff development, quality culture, learner wellbeing, institutional strategy and stakeholder responsibility.
sections.edufi_framing: EDUFI-aligned language connects this module to local curriculum development, equality, school culture, learner support, teacher professionalism and the translation of curriculum aims into daily practice. In FCEI terms, leaders support the conditions that allow curriculum goals to become visible through routines, teaching practice, learner participation, staff collaboration and evidence-informed reflection.
sections.fineec_framing: FINEEC-benchmarked language connects this module to enhancement-led evaluation and quality management. Enhancement-led development focuses on participation, trust, learning, appreciation, strengths, development targets and impact. Leaders are guided to use evidence as a tool for shared learning and improvement rather than blame or inspection alone.
sections.meaning: In practice, the module helps leaders establish routines such as leadership evidence walks, staff reflection meetings, learner voice reviews, quality culture check-ins, school improvement priority mapping, curriculum implementation reviews and wellbeing support reviews.
sections.why_matters: This matters because leadership quality shapes how teachers experience professional trust, how learners experience support and wellbeing, and how schools make improvement decisions. Strong leadership makes improvement visible, manageable and shared.
sections.context: The module can be used in schools, colleges, TVET centres, training providers, international schools, private institutions, community learning organisations and education projects. It is suitable for principals, senior leadership teams, owners, department heads, quality leads, teacher leaders and institutional development teams.
sections.evidence_requirement: Participants must submit one leadership improvement artefact showing priority, evidence, leadership action, staff or learner participation, reflection and improvement decision. The evidence must show the link: priority → evidence → leadership action → reflection → improvement decision.
sections.download_explanation: The core module includes practical templates that allow participants to diagnose quality culture, map improvement priorities, review evidence, plan action and record reflection. The paid add-on toolkit provides deeper implementation templates for whole-school rollout, reporting and stakeholder communication.
sections.reflection_prompt: What leadership priority did you identify? What evidence helped you understand the issue? How did the process support trust, participation or professional dialogue? What did you learn about your institution’s quality culture? What is the next improvement step?
sections.learner_phrasing: In this module, you will explore how school leadership can strengthen quality culture and support enhancement-led development. You will learn how to identify a leadership priority, use evidence responsibly, involve staff or learners, plan an improvement action and produce a practical leadership artefact for your professional evidence portfolio.
sections.cms_wording: Use this module as an LMS learning module inside C10. It requires login, progress tracking, video access, downloadable templates, knowledge check, action task, evidence upload, reflection and certificate evidence rule. Do not merge this learning module with the public consultancy service page.
sections.certificate_rule: The module is complete when the participant opens the content, watches or opens the learning video, downloads at least one core template, completes the knowledge check, submits the action task, uploads a leadership improvement artefact and completes the reflection prompt.
sections.certificate_statement: The participant has completed School Leadership, Quality Culture and Enhancement-Led Development within the FCEI Finnish Teacher Training Development Framework and has submitted evidence of leadership reflection, quality culture review and enhancement-led improvement planning.

terminology.definition: The process of guiding teaching, learning, staff development, school culture and institutional improvement through clear direction, professional support and responsible decision-making.
A shared institutional habit where staff review practice, use evidence, discuss improvement and take responsibility for better learning conditions.
An improvement approach focused on trust, participation, strengths, evidence, reflection, appreciation and sustainable development.
A school or institution’s structured review of its own practice using evidence, staff reflection, learner voice and improvement priorities.
Leadership decisions based on fair evidence, professional judgement and careful interpretation rather than assumption or blame.
A leadership culture where teachers are respected as professionals and supported to improve through dialogue, evidence and shared responsibility.
A repeated process of identifying a priority, planning action, collecting evidence, reflecting and deciding the next step.
Structured ways for learners to contribute information about their learning experience, participation, support, challenge and wellbeing.
Involving relevant people such as staff, learners, parents, governors, employers or partners in improvement decisions where appropriate.
The system by which the provider maintains, reviews and improves the quality of teaching, learning, support and institutional routines.

knowledge_check.question: What is the main purpose of enhancement-led development?
options: To support improvement through participation, trust, evidence, reflection and practical action
To remove the need for evidence
knowledge_check.answer: To support improvement through participation, trust, evidence, reflection and practical action

knowledge_check.question: What is quality culture?
options: A set of posters about school values
A shared institutional habit of reviewing practice, using evidence and improving learning conditions
knowledge_check.answer: A shared institutional habit of reviewing practice, using evidence and improving learning conditions

knowledge_check.question: What should leaders avoid when using evidence?
options: Using evidence to blame individuals without context, support or professional dialogue
Using evidence to decide next steps
knowledge_check.answer: Using evidence to blame individuals without context, support or professional dialogue

knowledge_check.question: Which phrase best fits FINEEC-benchmarked improvement?
What should a certificate evidence artefact show?
options: Priority, evidence, action, reflection and improvement decision
knowledge_check.answer: Priority, evidence, action, reflection and improvement decision

model_completed_example.context: A senior leadership team notices that staff meetings focus heavily on compliance tasks, but teachers rarely discuss evidence of learner support, feedback or wellbeing. Teachers report that improvement priorities are unclear and feedback from learners is not systematically used.
model_completed_example.priority: Strengthen quality culture by creating one monthly evidence-informed professional dialogue routine.
model_completed_example.baseline_evidence: Staff survey comments, meeting agendas, sample feedback records and learner voice responses show that staff want clearer improvement routines and more practical discussion of teaching and learner experience.
model_completed_example.leadership_action: Introduce a 45-minute monthly quality culture meeting using a fixed agenda: one strength, one learner voice finding, one practice challenge, one evidence source, one next action and one owner.
model_completed_example.evidence_collected: Completed staff dialogue records, learner voice summaries, revised meeting agendas and leadership reflection logs.
model_completed_example.improvement_decision: Continue the routine for one term, train department heads to facilitate it and add one learner support evidence review every month.

resources.title: Leadership Quality Culture Self-Review
resources.purpose: Review leadership routines, trust, participation, evidence use and improvement culture.

resources.purpose: Record the evidence behind a leadership decision and the next improvement step.

resources.title: Staff Voice and Professional Dialogue Template
resources.purpose: Structure staff input around strengths, concerns, opportunities and practical next steps.

resources.title: Learner Experience Review Form
resources.purpose: Gather learner evidence about participation, support, challenge, wellbeing and feedback.

resources.title: Enhancement-Led Development Action Plan
resources.purpose: Plan a manageable action that links priority, evidence, participation, timeline and review.

resources.title: Leadership Reflection and Improvement Log
resources.purpose: Record what changed, what was learned and what should happen next.

resources.title: Certificate Evidence Checklist

templates.title: Senior Leadership Team Reflection Pack
